trade.summary: Arabia Saudita is Corea del Sud's largest source of crude oil, accounting for approximately 30% of Corea del Sud's oil imports. Corea del Sud exports petrochemicals, automobiles (Hyundai-Kia), electronics, and construction services to Arabia Saudita. Corea del Sudn construction conglomerates have built major infrastructure across the Kingdom. The relationship is strongly asymmetric, shaped by Corea del Sud's energy import dependence.

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Saudi relations are primarily energy-driven but have expanded into Vision 2030 collaboration. Arabia Saudita sees Corea del Sud as a technology and construction partner for its diversification goals. Corea del Sud has participated in NEOM and other megaproject construction. Both sides maintain respectful relations and there is growing people-to-people connection through Corea del Sudn culture (K-pop, K-drama have significant Saudi followings).

military.summary: Corea del Sud has expanded defense exports globally and Arabia Saudita is a potential market. Corea del Sudn defense systems (K9 howitzers, coastal defense) have been marketed in Arabia Saudita. No formal defense treaty. Corea del Sud's focus remains on the Corea del Sudn Peninsula deterrence mission. Arabia Saudita purchases primarily Stati Uniti, European, and Regno Unito weapons.

financial.summary: Saudi Aramco has a strategic stake in S-OIL, one of Corea del Sud's largest refiners, ensuring downstream processing of Saudi crude in Corea del Sud. Saudi PIF has made investments in Corea del Sudn companies and funds. Corea del Sudn construction conglomerates (Samsung C&T, Hyundai E&C, SK) have generated enormous revenues from Saudi projects, with new Vision 2030 projects continuing to flow.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita is Corea del Sud's number one crude oil supplier, a relationship of paramount strategic importance. Saudi Aramco's ownership stake in S-OIL refinery in Corea del Sud deepens this relationship. Both countries are exploring hydrogen cooperation as Arabia Saudita positions for green hydrogen exports. The energy relationship is the anchor of the bilateral.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation driven by Vision 2030. Corea del Sud-Saudi research ties are developing in renewable energy, desalination, and digital technology. KAStati UnitiT-Corea del Sudn university partnerships are growing. Corea del Sudn companies bring technology transfer as part of Vision 2030 localization requirements.
